heading: Compound Wildcard Search and Sort Example
body:
The following query shows how to query and sort the results by a
string field. It searches for titles that begin with `Prance` or
`Prince` and sorts the results by the `title` field in ascending
order.

The query uses the following pipeline stages:

- [$search](pipe.$search) to search the `title` field
  using the `should` clause with the [wildcard](wildcard-ref) operator to
  search for titles that begin with `Prance` and `Prince`.
  The query also specifies that results must be sorted by the `title`
  field in ascending order.

- [$limit](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/limit) stage to limit the output to `5` results.

- [$project](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/project) stage to:

  - Exclude all fields except `title`.

  - Add a field named `score`.

Atlas UI

The MongoDB Search results contain documents with movie titles that begin with
`Prance` and `Prince`. MongoDB Search returns titles with `Prance`
followed by `Prince` because MongoDB Search sorts documents by the `title`
field in ascending order.
